description: Multi-turn evals for the interview-coach agent. Each test describes a complete mock interview session: the prompt sets up the company/role/round context, expected_output describes what a complete session should produce, and assertions check structural properties across the multi-turn session.

context.research.md: Acme Corp: Series C, 300 employees. Product: AcmeData, analytics platform. Engineering blog active, recently migrated to Kafka. Culture: data-driven, remote-first, high ownership.
context.jd.md: Role: Senior Software Engineer. Requirements: Python (5+ years), distributed systems, Kafka or messaging queues, API design, cross-functional collaboration. Nice to have: Go, Kubernetes.
context.team.md: Hiring Manager: Sarah Chen, VP of Engineering.
context.profile.md:
Name: Alex Rivera
Work Experience:
- Senior Software Engineer at FinCo (2021–present): Built real-time data pipelines processing 5M events/day using Kafka and Python
- Software Engineer at StartupX (2018–2021): Shipped core product features, reduced API latency 40%
Skills: Python, Kafka, PostgreSQL, distributed systems, REST APIs

simulated_turns.agent_action: Reads context files, asks which round to practice
Explains rules (one question at a time, feedback after each), asks if ready
Asks first question: Tell me about yourself / walk me through your background
simulated_turns.user_response: I'm a senior backend engineer with 8 years of experience, most recently at FinCo where I built a real-time data pipeline handling 5 million events per day using Kafka and Python. Before that I was at StartupX where I reduced API latency by 40%. I'm drawn to Acme because of your migration to Kafka — I'd love to help scale that further.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides structured feedback (Strong / Improve / Tip), then asks next question
simulated_turns.user_response: At FinCo we had a Kafka cluster that was falling behind during peak traffic — millions of events queued up. I identified that our consumer group wasn't balanced across partitions. I repartitioned the topic and optimized the consumer configuration, which eliminated the backlog within an hour and reduced average processing latency from 800ms to 50ms.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides structured feedback, asks next question (behavioral or role-specific)
simulated_turns.user_response: I try to build shared context first — I write a short doc explaining the problem, the options, and my recommendation. Then I set up a 30-minute call to get input before deciding. At StartupX I used this when we were choosing between REST and GraphQL for a new API. It helped surface a constraint the product team had that changed my recommendation.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides structured feedback, continues with remaining questions through turn 12 (8–10 questions total)
Closes mock interview, delivers full debrief with Overall Assessment, Strengths, Development Areas, Top 3 Answers to Polish, Recommended Practice
Writes debrief to tracking.md

tests.expected_output: A complete mock interview session for a hiring manager round at Acme Corp. Agent reads context files before starting. Asks one question at a time (8–10 questions). Provides structured feedback (Strong / Improve / Tip) after each answer. Questions are tailored to the hiring manager round and Acme's context (Kafka, data pipelines, cross-functional collaboration). Session closes with a full debrief covering Overall Assessment, Strengths, Development Areas, Top 3 Answers to Polish, and Recommended Practice. When user says yes, debrief is written to tracking.md.
assertions: Agent reads context files (research.md, jd.md, team.md, profile.md) before asking the first question
Agent asks which round to practice before starting the interview
Agent explains the format (one question at a time, feedback after each) before asking the first question
Agent asks exactly one question per turn — never bundles multiple questions
Agent provides structured feedback after every answer, including Strong, Improve, and Tip fields
At least one question references Acme Corp specifically (Kafka migration, analytics platform, etc.)
At least one behavioral question is asked (requiring STAR-format answer)
Total questions asked is between 8 and 10
Agent delivers a closing debrief after the final question
Debrief includes Overall Assessment (2–3 sentences), Strengths, Development Areas, Top 3 Answers to Polish, and Recommended Practice
When user confirms, debrief is written to tracking.md under '## Mock Interview Debrief'

context.research.md: TechGiant: Public company, 50K employees. Open-sourced load balancing library (2K stars). Known for distributed tracing and reliability engineering. Culture: technical excellence, high autonomy.
context.jd.md: Role: Staff Engineer, Platform. Requirements: 10+ years experience, distributed systems architecture, Go, Kubernetes, technical leadership, cross-team collaboration. Must-have: systems design at scale.
context.team.md: Panel: Maria Lopez (Principal Engineer), Dev Patel (Staff Engineer), Anna Kim (Engineering Manager).
context.profile.md:
Name: Jamie Wong
Work Experience:
- Principal Engineer at CloudCo (2019–present): Distributed caching system, 99.99% uptime across 12 regions
- Senior Engineer at WebCo (2016–2019): Monolith-to-microservices migration, reduced deploy time 80%
Skills: Go, distributed systems, Kubernetes, systems design, technical leadership

simulated_turns.agent_action: Reads context files, asks which round to practice
Explains format, asks if ready
Asks first systems design question appropriate to Staff Engineer technical round
simulated_turns.user_response: I'd start by understanding the access patterns — read-heavy or write-heavy, consistency requirements, expected QPS. For a distributed cache at this scale I'd use consistent hashing for partition assignment so we can add nodes without rebalancing the full keyspace. We'd need replication for availability — at least 3 replicas per shard. I'd also design a circuit breaker pattern so cache failures degrade gracefully to the underlying store.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides structured feedback, asks follow-up or next question
simulated_turns.user_response: At CloudCo we had an incident where a single slow downstream call caused thread pool exhaustion across the entire service mesh. I implemented adaptive timeouts and bulkhead patterns, which isolated the failure. Going forward I added canary analysis for every deploy to catch latency regressions before full rollout.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides structured feedback, continues through 8–10 technical questions
Delivers full debrief focused on technical depth, systems design, and leadership signals

tests.expected_output: A complete mock technical interview for a Staff Engineer role at TechGiant. Agent asks systems design, distributed systems, and technical leadership questions appropriate to a Staff+ level. Feedback after each answer evaluates technical depth and communication clarity. Debrief highlights systems design strengths and any gaps in leadership storytelling. Agent does not write to tracking.md since user declined.
assertions: Agent reads context files before starting
Agent asks which round to practice before starting
Agent asks only one question per turn
Questions are appropriate for a technical/Staff Engineer round (systems design, distributed systems, Go/Kubernetes)
At least one question involves a systems design or architecture scenario
Structured feedback (Strong / Improve / Tip) provided after every answer
Total questions asked is between 8 and 10
Debrief evaluates technical depth, not just communication
Debrief includes Top 3 Answers to Polish with specific systems design feedback
When user declines to save, agent does NOT write to tracking.md
Agent does not use Edit tool (disallowedTools compliance)

context.research.md: StartupCo: Series A, 30 engineers, 80 employees. B2B SaaS for project management. Fast-moving, engineering-driven. Raised $15M.
context.jd.md: Role: Senior Backend Engineer. Requirements: Node.js (3+ years), PostgreSQL, microservices or SOA experience, fast-paced startup environment. Nice to have: GraphQL, Redis.
context.team.md: Recruiter: Lisa Park. Hiring Manager: James Wu, Head of Engineering.
context.profile.md:
Name: Jordan Kim
Work Experience:
- Senior Backend Engineer at ScaleCo (2020–present): Microservices migration, reduced deploy time 80%, Node.js, PostgreSQL
- Engineer at AgencyX (2018–2020): REST APIs for 3 enterprise clients
Skills: Node.js, PostgreSQL, microservices, REST APIs, Docker

simulated_turns.agent_action: Reads context files, asks which round to practice
Explains format, asks if ready. Notes this will be lighter — recruiter screens focus on background, motivation, and basic fit.
Asks recruiter screen question: 'Can you walk me through your background and what brought you to apply to StartupCo?'
simulated_turns.user_response: I've been a senior backend engineer for about 6 years, most recently at ScaleCo where I led a monolith-to-microservices migration. I'm looking for a startup environment where I can have more ownership and see the direct impact of my work. StartupCo caught my eye because of the Series A momentum and the engineering-driven culture.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Provides feedback, asks about motivation and timeline
simulated_turns.user_response: I'm actively looking and have a couple of conversations in progress. I'm hoping to make a move within the next 4–6 weeks.

simulated_turns.agent_action: Continues with recruiter screen questions (compensation range, work authorization, availability) for 8 total questions
Delivers debrief appropriate for recruiter screen: focuses on pitch clarity, conciseness, enthusiasm, and follow-up questions the candidate asked
Writes debrief to tracking.md

tests.expected_output: A complete mock recruiter screen for a backend engineer role at StartupCo. Questions are appropriate for a recruiter screen (background walkthrough, motivation, timeline, work authorization, salary range, culture fit). Questions are lighter than a technical or panel round. Feedback focuses on pitch clarity, conciseness, and enthusiasm rather than technical depth. Debrief reflects recruiter screen dynamics. Debrief saved to tracking.md when user confirms.
assertions: Agent reads context files before starting
Agent calibrates questions to recruiter screen format (background, motivation, timeline, logistics — not deep technical)
Agent asks only one question per turn
Questions do not include systems design or deep technical problems
At least one question covers motivation for applying to StartupCo specifically
Structured feedback (Strong / Improve / Tip) provided after every answer
Total questions asked is between 8 and 10
Debrief evaluates pitch clarity and conciseness, not technical depth
When user confirms, debrief is written to tracking.md
Agent does not use Edit tool (disallowedTools compliance)
